title: verbascum cibinense
intro: Verbascum cibinense, also known as Tibetan mullein, is a flowering plant species native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas. Traditionally used in traditional medicine, Verbascum cibinense is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a demulcent and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps soothe and calm irritated tissues and reduce inflammation. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. In herbal preparations, Verbascum cibinense is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically to the skin to benefit from its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
